name: Sierra Ahillos and Víboras Reservoir Loop
name_original: Alcaudete – vuelta por las Miradores del Víboras
description: This difficult 26.4-mile road cycling route around Alcaudete offers great views and a challenging ride through olive groves.
region_intro: Road cycling around Alcaudete offers diverse landscapes, from rolling hills covered in olive groves to the more challenging ascents of the Sub-Baetic mountains. The region features the Vía Verde del Aceite, a dedicated path for cycling, alongside varied topography that includes steep slopes and flatter areas. Cyclists can explore routes that traverse ancient olive groves, cross historical bridges, and pass by natural reserves. This blend of terrain provides options for various road cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around Alcaudete The most popular road cycling route is Sierra Ahillos and Víboras Reservoir Loop, a 26.4 miles (42.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 33 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the Sierra Ahillos and passes by the Víboras Reservoir. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Alcaudete and Víboras Reservoir Loop, a moderate 24.0 miles (38.7 km) path. This route offers views of Alcaudete and the Víboras Reservoir. Local road cyclists also love the Castillo de Alcaudete – Greenway Tunnel loop from Alcaudete, a 62.1 miles (99.9 km) trail leading through the Vía Verde del Aceite, often completed in about 5 hours 41 minutes. Road cycling around Alcaudete is defined by endless olive groves, rolling hills, and sections of the Vía Verde del Aceite. The network offers options for easy rides, moderate loops, and more challenging climbs. The routes in Alcaudete are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. More than 390 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Alcaudete's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Alcaudete?
A: Alcaudete offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 35 tours available on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, including 13 moderate and 24 difficult options, ensuring there's a suitable ride for every road cyclist.

Q: What kind of terrain and difficulty can I expect on road cycling routes in Alcaudete?
A: The region presents a diverse landscape for road cycling. You'll encounter rolling hills covered in endless olive groves, sections of the relatively flat &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27630921" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Aceite&lt;/a&gt;, and more challenging ascents into the Sub-Baetic mountains, such as those found on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996231437/sierra-ahillos-and-viboras-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Ahillos and Víboras Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;. Routes range from moderate to difficult, with significant elevation changes on many climbs.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling routes in Alcaudete?
A: While many routes in Alcaudete feature challenging climbs, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27630921" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Aceite&lt;/a&gt; offers a dedicated, mostly flat path that is ideal for families. This old railway line is free from vehicle traffic, providing a safe and enjoyable environment for cyclists of all ages to experience the region's natural beauty.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I see while road cycling around Alcaudete?
A: Road cycling routes in Alcaudete are rich with scenic views and historical points of interest. You can expect to pass through vast olive groves, alongside the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5312423" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Víboras Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and near the ecologically significant &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7365955" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Honda&lt;/a&gt;. Historical highlights include the majestic Calatravo Castle, impressive iron viaducts, and a Roman bridge over the Víboras River, particularly along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27630921" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Aceite&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is parking available near the road cycling routes in Alcaudete?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in Alcaudete, especially near access points for popular routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27630921" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Aceite&lt;/a&gt;. Many towns along the greenway, such as Alcaudete itself, offer designated parking areas for visitors and cyclists.

Q: Can I reach the road cycling routes in Alcaudete using public transport with my bike?
A: Public transport options in rural Andalusia can be limited, and policies regarding bicycle transport on buses vary. It's advisable to check with local bus operators in Alcaudete directly for their specific regulations on carrying bicycles before planning your journey.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Alcaudete?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally considered the best for road cycling in Alcaudete. During these seasons, the weather is typically mild and pleasant, making for comfortable riding conditions. Summers can be very hot, while winters are cooler but still offer opportunities for cycling, especially on sunny days.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the Vía Verde del Aceite?
A: Yes, dogs are generally permitted on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27630921" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Aceite&lt;/a&gt;, provided they are kept on a leash. This allows cyclists to enjoy the scenic route with their canine companions, though it's always good practice to ensure your dog is comfortable with longer rides.

Q: Are there any cafes or restaurants along the road cycling routes in Alcaudete?
A: Yes, you'll find various cafes and restaurants in Alcaudete and in the smaller towns along the cycling routes. Notably, some old railway stations along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27630921" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Aceite&lt;/a&gt; have been repurposed into restaurants or interpretation centers, offering convenient stops for refreshments and local cuisine.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Alcaudete?
A: The road cycling experience in Alcaudete is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists frequently praise the region's diverse terrain, from the unique experience of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27630921" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Aceite&lt;/a&gt; to the challenging climbs and panoramic views of the olive groves and mountains.

Q: Are there road cycling routes in Alcaudete that go beyond the Vía Verde del Aceite?
A: Absolutely. While the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27630921" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Aceite&lt;/a&gt; is a highlight, Alcaudete offers numerous other road cycling routes that explore the broader region. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19380707" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Alcalá la Real loop from Alcaudete&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33782645" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Castillo – View of Olive Groves loop&lt;/a&gt; take you through varied landscapes, including rolling hills and more demanding mountain passes.

Q: What is the surface type of the Vía Verde del Aceite and is it suitable for road bikes?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27630921" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Aceite&lt;/a&gt; is an old railway line converted into a greenway, typically featuring a compacted gravel or asphalt surface. While it's generally suitable for road bikes, especially those with slightly wider tires, cyclists should be aware that some sections might be rougher than paved roads. It's a popular choice for touring and leisure cycling.
tips: Hydroelectric dam on the northern slope of Sierra las Víboras. The dam on the west side of the reservoir collects the waters of the Víboras River.

Considered one of the most fought-over fortresses in the region, the castle dominates the town located on the old road from the Guadalquivir River to Granada. It is worth the climb for the spectacular views of the surrounding landscape and mountains. The Alcaudete castle has a museum that highlights the history of the Military Order of Calatrava.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as difficult and requires good fitness due to its distance of approximately 42.5 km and an elevation gain of around 909 meters. It is best suited for experienced road cyclists.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily designed for racebikes, meaning you can expect mostly well-paved surfaces. However, some segments in the wider Alcaudete area, even those classified as moderate or hard, may include unpaved or more challenging sections. Always be prepared for varied road conditions.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest and scenic views along the route?
faqs.answer: This tour offers spectacular views of the surrounding landscape. You'll enjoy the View of Embalse del Víboras and the historic View of Alcaudete Castle, which overlooks the town. Other notable features include the historic Viaducto del río Víboras (Puente Eiffel) and picturesque olive groves.

faqs.question: Where can I park and start this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: While the exact starting point for parking isn't specified, Alcaudete town offers various parking options. Many cyclists access routes in the region from convenient points within the town or near sections of the Vía Verde del Aceite. It's advisable to check local parking availability upon arrival.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle in the Alcaudete region is typically during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is generally milder and more pleasant for cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential chill of winter.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, generally, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle this route. It utilizes public roads and sections of the Vía Verde del Aceite, which are freely accessible.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As a racebike route primarily on roads and paved paths, it's generally not ideal for dogs to accompany cyclists directly on the trail. If you plan to bring a dog, it would be best to ensure they are safely secured in a bike trailer designed for pets, or consider alternative walking trails in the area.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other paths. Notably, it includes parts of the Vía Verde del Aceite, a popular greenway converted from an old railway line. It also briefly touches upon the Camino Mozárabe and the Camino Natural Vía Verde del Aceite: Jaén a rio Guadajoz.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants along the way?
faqs.answer: The route passes through or near Alcaudete, where you can find various cafes, restaurants, and other amenities to refuel. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially for longer stretches between towns.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this racebike tour?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, you should bring your racebike in good condition, a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, sufficient water, energy snacks, a basic repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers), and a fully charged phone. Sunscreen and sunglasses are also recommended, especially during sunnier months.

faqs.question: Is the route a loop or an out-and-back?
faqs.answer: This specific tour, "Alcaudete - tour around the Viboras Viewpoints," is designed as a loop, bringing you back to your starting point. This allows for a continuous and varied cycling experience without needing to retrace your steps.
